title: HTML Basics
description: Learn the building blocks of HTML and create your first web page

blocks.content:
HTML stands for **HyperText Markup Language**. It's the standard markup language used to create web pages.

Think of HTML as the skeleton of a webpage:
- It provides structure
- It organizes content (headings, paragraphs, links, images)
- It tells the browser what each part of the page is

HTML uses **tags** to mark up content. Tags are keywords surrounded by angle brackets `< >`.

Most tags come in pairs:
- **Opening tag**: `<tagname>`
- **Closing tag**: `</tagname>`
- **Content**: Text or other elements go between them

This is a paragraph of text.

This is a heading

This is a link
Every HTML document needs this basic structure. It's like a template you use for every page:

- `<!DOCTYPE html>` - Tells the browser this is HTML5
- `<html>` - Root element that wraps everything
- `<head>` - Contains information about the page (title, links, metadata)
- `<title>` - The name shown in the browser tab
- `<body>` - Contains all visible content on the page

**Headings** (for titles and section headers): - `

Welcome to My Website!

Hello! This is my first web page.

About Me

I'm learning HTML and CSS to build websites.

My Interests

  • Web Development
  • Learning New Technologies
  • Creating Cool Projects

Check out my favorite website: Example.com
Here's what that code looks like in a real browser — a live preview:

blocks.html:
Welcome to My Website!

Hello! This is my first web page.

About Me

I'm learning HTML and CSS to build websites.

My Interests

  • Web Development
  • Learning New Technologies
  • Creating Cool Projects

Check out my favorite website: Example.com
blocks.caption: Live preview: what the HTML looks like in a browser

blocks.content:
1. Open your text editor
2. Copy the code from "Your First HTML Page" above
3. Save it as `index.html` (the .html extension is important!)
4. Open the file in your web browser
5. You should see your webpage!

**Try modifying it:**
- Change the title
- Add more paragraphs
- Add more list items
- Change the heading text
💾 **Remember**: Save your HTML files with a `.html` extension. This tells your browser it's a web page!
📌 In the next section, you'll learn CSS to make your page look beautiful. HTML is just the structure - CSS adds the style!
